message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent. Confirm this fix is BOTH appropriate (correct, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (the named test DISCRIMINATES — fails if reverted). Read the actual changed file + test at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
Fix CV-O-1 in scripts/capture_transcripts.py
What: _copy_scrubbed scrubs EVERY text file (decode-strict; binary copied verbatim), not only .jsonl — closes the .json workflow-transcript PII/secret bypass. Working tree re-scrubbed by the pre-commit hook (0 raw-username occurrences); git history needs filter-repo before publish (documented).
Test(s): test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_non_jsonl_text_files, test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim
Verdict CONFIRMED or CONCERN; be specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

100	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
101	    try:
102	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
103	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()
104	        # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
105	        # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
106	        # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
107	        # Decode strictly to classify: text → scrub; genuinely binary (rare here) → copy verbatim.
108	        try:
109	            text = raw.decode("utf-8")
110	        except UnicodeDecodeError:
111	            data = raw  # binary blob — nothing to scrub
112	        else:
113	            data = _scrub(text).encode("utf-8")
114	        if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
115	            pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
116	        else:
117	            dstf.write_bytes(data)
118	        return {
119	            "path": str(dstf.relative_to(DEST.parent.parent)),
120	            "bytes": len(data),
121	            "sha256": hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest(),
122	        }
123	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — provenance capture must never break a commit
124	        return None
125	
126	
127	def main() -> int:
128	    src = Path(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC", str(DEFAULT_SRC)))
129	    if not src.is_dir():
130	        return 0
131	    session = _active_session(src)
132	    if session is None:
133	        return 0
134	    sid = session.stem
135	    captured: list[dict] = []
136	
137	    rec = _copy_scrubbed(session, DEST / f"{sid}.jsonl")
138	    if rec:
139	        captured.append(rec)
140	
141	    # the session's subtree: subagents/ + workflows/ transcripts
142	    subtree = src / sid
143	    if subtree.is_dir():
144	        for f in sorted(subtree.rglob("*")):
145	            if f.is_file():
146	                rel = f.relative_to(subtree)
147	                rec = _copy_scrubbed(f, DEST / sid / rel)
148	                if rec:
149	                    captured.append(rec)
150	
151	    try:
152	        MANIFEST.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
153	        MANIFEST.write_text(
154	            json.dumps(
155	                {
156	                    "captured_at": _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
157	                    "active_session": sid,
158	                    "n_files": len(captured),
159	                    "total_bytes": sum(c["bytes"] for c in captured),
160	                    "files": captured,
161	                },
162	                indent=2,
163	            )
164	            + "\n",

95	    # ANY length (the bare-token rule skips short handles to avoid over-redaction, but the slug
96	    # form is unambiguous PII).
97	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_slug")
98	    out = cap._scrub("dir: -Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc and -home-[REDACTED]-proj")  # short names
99	    assert "-Users-[REDACTED]-" not in out and "-home-[REDACTED]-" not in out
100	    assert "-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc" in out and "-home-[REDACTED]-proj" in out
101	
102	
103	def test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_non_jsonl_text_files(tmp_path):
104	    # #CV-O-1: the session subtree holds .json workflow/subagent transcripts; redaction must apply
105	    # to EVERY text file, not only .jsonl, or operator PII + secrets leak into committed files.
106	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_nonjsonl")
107	    src = tmp_path / "wf.json"  # a .json (NOT .jsonl) workflow transcript
108	    src.write_text('{"path": "/Users/[REDACTED]/x", "tok": "[REDACTED]"}', "utf-8")
109	    dst = tmp_path / "out" / "wf.json"
110	    cap._copy_scrubbed(src, dst)
111	    out = dst.read_text("utf-8")
112	    assert "somebody" not in out and "/Users/[REDACTED]/x" in out  # path username scrubbed
113	    assert "abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv" not in out  # bearer token scrubbed
114	
115	
116	def test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim(tmp_path):
117	    # #CV-O-1: a genuinely binary file (undecodable) is copied unchanged, not corrupted.
118	    cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_bin")
119	    src = tmp_path / "blob.bin"
120	    src.write_bytes(b"\xff\xfe\x00\x01binary")
121	    dst = tmp_path / "out" / "blob.bin"
122	    cap._copy_scrubbed(src, dst)
123	    assert dst.read_bytes() == b"\xff\xfe\x00\x01binary"

130	    assert b"example-study-secret-key".decode() not in src  # the stale weak literal is gone
131	    assert "os.urandom(32)" in src  # the fresh-per-run secret is in place

content.text: The diff confirms the old code gated scrubbing on `suffix == ".jsonl"` and copied everything else raw. Now let me run the tests with python3 and verify discrimination by reverting.
Both named tests pass. Now let me verify discrimination by reverting the fix and confirming the non-jsonl test fails.

p="scripts/capture_transcripts.py"
s=open(p).read()
old='''        # Scrub EVERY text file, not only .jsonl (#CV-O-1): the session subtree also holds .json
        # workflow/subagent transcripts that carry the same operator PII + credential-shaped
        # secrets — the old suffix==".jsonl" gate copied those RAW, bypassing redaction entirely.
        # Decode strictly to classify: text → scrub; genuinely binary (rare here) → copy verbatim.
        try:
            text = raw.decode("utf-8")
        except UnicodeDecodeError:
            data = raw  # binary blob — nothing to scrub
        else:
            data = _scrub(text).encode("utf-8")'''

            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
        else:
            data = raw'''
assert old in s
open(p,"w").write(s.replace(old,new))
print("reverted")

content.content:
def test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_non_jsonl_text_files(tmp_path):
        # #CV-O-1: the session subtree holds .json workflow/subagent transcripts; redaction must apply
        # to EVERY text file, not only .jsonl, or operator PII + secrets leak into committed files.
        cap = _load("scripts/capture_transcripts.py", "capture_transcripts_nonjsonl")
        src = tmp_path / "wf.json"  # a .json (NOT .jsonl) workflow transcript
        src.write_text('{"path": "/Users/[REDACTED]/x", "tok": "[REDACTED]"}', "utf-8")
        dst = tmp_path / "out" / "wf.json"
        cap._copy_scrubbed(src, dst)
        out = dst.read_text("utf-8")
>       assert "somebody" not in out and "/Users/[REDACTED]/x" in out  # path username scrubbed

E         'somebody' is contained here:

E         ?                  ++++++++)

content.text:
Discrimination confirmed: the non-jsonl test fails on revert (leaks `somebody`), passes with the fix. The binary test passes both ways (correct — binary handling is unchanged for binary input, it just documents the verbatim-copy guarantee for the new decode path).

Let me confirm the file is fully restored and the broader suite is green.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git diff --quiet scripts/capture_transcripts.py && echo "CLEAN - matches committed" && python3 -m pytest tests/test_scripts.py -q 2>&1 | tail -5
input.description: Confirm restore and run full test_scripts

content.text:
Verification complete. File restored cleanly, full test_scripts.py green.

Assessment:

**Appropriate:** The fix replaces the `suffix == ".jsonl"` gate (which copied all other files — including `.json` workflow/subagent transcripts — RAW) with a decode-strict classification: any UTF-8-decodable file gets scrubbed via `_scrub`, genuinely binary files (UnicodeDecodeError) are copied verbatim. This correctly closes the PII/secret bypass. It's idiomatic (try/except/else, decode strict rather than `errors="replace"` so binary is reliably detected). No regression: jsonl files still scrubbed; binary files preserved byte-for-byte; the broad `except Exception` provenance-safety wrapper and manifest reporting are untouched. The decode strategy is actually an improvement over the old `errors="replace"` which could silently corrupt non-UTF8 jsonl.

**Complete (discriminates):**
- `test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_non_jsonl_text_files`: FAILS when reverted (leaks `somebody` + bearer token from a `.json` file), PASSES with fix. Strong discriminator targeting the exact bypass.
- `test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim`: passes both ways since binary input was always copied raw — it's a guard/documentation test for the new decode branch, not a discriminator. That's fine: the first test carries discrimination for the security-relevant change.

One minor note (not a concern): the git-history filter-repo cleanup is documented as out-of-scope follow-up, consistent with the fix description. The working-tree re-scrub is the in-scope deliverable and is verified by the discriminating test.

input.reasoning:
Verified at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. The fix in scripts/capture_transcripts.py:_copy_scrubbed (lines 100-124) replaces the old `if srcf.suffix == ".jsonl"` gate — which copied every non-jsonl file (incl. .json workflow/subagent transcripts) RAW, bypassing redaction — with a decode-strict classification: UTF-8-decodable text is run through _scrub, genuinely binary files (UnicodeDecodeError) are copied verbatim. This correctly closes the .json PII/secret bypass.

APPROPRIATE: correct, no regression (jsonl still scrubbed; binary preserved byte-for-byte; the broad except-Exception provenance guard and manifest logic untouched), and idiomatic (try/except/else; strict decode is actually safer than the prior errors="replace" which could silently mangle bytes). The history filter-repo cleanup is correctly scoped out and documented; working-tree re-scrub is the in-scope deliverable.

COMPLETE / DISCRIMINATES: I reverted to the original suffix-gate code and re-ran. test_copy_scrubbed_scrubs_non_jsonl_text_files FAILS on revert (leaks 'somebody' + bearer token from a .json file) and PASSES with the fix — a strong discriminator on the exact bypass. test_copy_scrubbed_copies_binary_verbatim passes both ways (binary was always copied raw) so it is a guard/documentation test, not a discriminator — acceptable since the first test carries the security-relevant discrimination. Both named tests pass on the committed code; full tests/test_scripts.py green (8 passed); file restored clean (git diff empty).

content.text:
Verification complete. Verdict: CONFIRMED.

The CV-O-1 fix in `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/scripts/capture_transcripts.py` is both appropriate and complete. The non-jsonl test discriminates (fails on revert, leaking the username and bearer token); the binary test is a guard/documentation test that passes both ways. File restored clean, full suite green.
